zh.lifeSummary: 當Margaret Barker於1501年出生在英國英格蘭格盧斯特斯爾朗 馬爾斯頓時，她的父親Rev John Barker39歲，母親Margaret Coleclough39歲。她於1525年與William De Deighton結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和2個女兒的父母。她去世於1585年2月23日在她的家鄉享年84歲。

nameMeanings.name: Barker
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: occupational name for a tanner of leather, from Middle English barkere ‘tanner’, tree bark having been used as the tanning agent.

English: occupational name for a shepherd, from Middle English berker, bercher (Old French berchier, bercher, berkier, berker, Late Latin berbicarius, from berbex ‘ram’, genitive berbicis). With the change of -ar- to -er- in Middle English, this became indistinguishable from the preceding name (see 1 above).
Americanized form of German   Berger   or   Barger  .

variants.name: Berger
meanings: German, Dutch, Swedish, and Jewish (Ashkenazic): topographic name for someone who lived in the mountains or hills (see   Berg  ). The surname of German origin is also found in many other European countries, e.g. in France (Alsace and Lorraine), Russia, Poland, Czechia, Hungary, and Croatia, and Slovenia, often as a translation into German of corresponding Slavic topographic names or surnames. As a Jewish name it is mainly artificial. Compare   Bargar  ,   Barger  ,   Barker  ,   Barrier  , and   Barriger  .
French: occupational name from Old French bergier ‘shepherd’ (from Late Latin berbicarius, from berbex ‘ram’). It is also found in England, as a surname of Huguenot origin. Compare Shepard .
Norwegian: habitational name from any of various farms so named with the plural of   Berg   ‘mountain’.

nameMeanings.name: Margaret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaretta
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

en.lifeSummary: When Margaret Barker was born in 1501, in Long Marston, Gloucestershire, England, United Kingdom, her father, Rev John Barker, was 39 and her mother, Margaret Coleclough, was 39. She married William De Deighton in 1525. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 2 daughters. She died on 23 February 1585, in her hometown, at the age of 84.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Margaret Barker est née en 1501, à Long Marston, Gloucestershire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Rev John Barker, avait 39 ans et sa mère, Margaret Coleclough, avait 39 ans. Elle a épousé William De Deighton en 1525. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 2 filles. Elle est décédée le 23 février 1585, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 84 ans.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Margaret Barker è nata il nel 1501 nel Long Marston, Gloucestershire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, sua padre, Rev John Barker, aveva 39 anni e sua madre, Margaret Coleclough, aveva 39 anni. Lei sposato William De Deighton nel 1525. Erano genitori di almeno 3 figli e 2 figlie. Morì il 23 febbraio 1585, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 84 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Margaret Barkerが1501年にイギリスイギリスグロウセステルシュアイアロング マーストンで生まれた時，父Rev John Barkerは39歳，母Margaret Colecloughは39歳であった。1525年にWilliam De Deightonと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼女は1585年2月23日に故郷で84歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Margaret Barker(이)가 1501년 영국 잉글랜드 그루스스터실르 론그 마스턴에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Rev John Barker(은)는 39세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Margaret Coleclough(은)는 39세였다. 그녀는 1525년 William De Deighton(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1585년 2월 23일 그녀의 고향에서 84세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Margaret Barker nasceu em 1501, em Long Marston, Gloucestershire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, seu pai, Rev John Barker, tinha 39 anos e sua mãe, Margaret Coleclough, tinha 39 anos. Ela casou-se com William De Deighton em 1525. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos e 2 filhas. Ela faleceu em 23 de fevereiro de 1585, em sua cidade natal, com 84 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Margaret Barker родилась в 1501 г. в местности лонг марстон, глоусестиршир, Англия, Великобритания, её отцу по имени Rev John Barker было 39 лет, а матери, которую звали Margaret Coleclough, 39. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени William De Deighton в 1525 г. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 2 дочери. Она умерла 23 февраля 1585 г. в её родном городе в возрасте 84 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Margaret Barker nació en 1501, en Long Marston, Gloucestershire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, su papá, Rev John Barker, tenía 39 años y su mamá, Margaret Coleclough, tenía 39 años. Se casó con William De Deighton en 1525. Fueron padres de por lo menos 3 hijos y 2 hijas. Murió el 23 de febrero de 1585, en su pueblo, a la edad de 84 años.
